title: columnea lehmannii
intro: Columnea lehmannii, also known as the hummingbird flower or hummingbird's trumpet, is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, where it is often found growing in the wild. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and has been used to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, cough, and digestive problems. Columnea lehmannii can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are made by extracting the plant's active compounds with water or a solvent.
